methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Quaise Energy is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Carlos Araque and Matt Houde that has raised $75M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set spanning one public company and eight private peers in the climate and energy sector, with primary focus on three same-stage private companies: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $75M total capital raised proxy, implying a $150M valuation. The peer-median method calculates the median valuation across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables, which equals $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. Both methods converge on identical point estimates, which increases confidence in the result.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, with a median point of $150M. The tight convergence between independent methods provides support for the central estimate. Key uncertainties include relative stage readiness and capital efficiency versus peers, unit economics of the core technology, and competitive positioning within the broader decarbonization technology landscape. Additional due diligence on technical validation milestones and go-to-market readiness would sharpen the range.
